---
title: "Initial Graphs"
author: "Kyran Lakhia"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r}
#Loading data/packages
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)

loan <- read_csv("lc_2016_2017.csv")
head(loan)
```


Question 5: Which states are experiencing the lowest grades on average?
```{r}
# Convert grades to numeric
loan_avg <- loan %>%
  mutate(grade_num = as.numeric(factor(grade, levels = c("A","B","C","D","E","F","G"))))

# Calculate average grade by state
state_avg <- loan_avg %>%
  group_by(addr_state) %>%
  summarise(avg_grade = mean(grade_num, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_grade)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

# Plot
ggplot(state_avg, aes(x = reorder(addr_state, avg_grade), y = avg_grade, fill = avg_grade)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#fcae91", high = "#cb181d") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = 1:7,
    labels = c("A","B","C","D","E","F","G")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "States with the Worst Average Loan Grades (2016–2017)",
    subtitle = "Loan grades range from A (best) to G (worst)",
    x = "State",
    y = "Average Loan Grade"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```
